items.title: LA School District Downgraded by Moody’s as Enrollment Shrinks
items.description: Los Angeles Unified School District’s credit grade was cut one level by Moody’s Ratings on Tuesday, as declining enrollment and growing costs pressure its finances.

items.title: NovaGold to Buy Billionaire Paulson’s Stake in Alaska Gold Mine
items.description: NovaGold Resources Inc. agreed to acquire billionaire investor John Paulson’s 40% stake in the Donlin Gold project in Alaska, giving the company full ownership of a giant undeveloped bullion mine.

items.title: Hormuz and Heat Trigger an Asian Scramble for LNG
items.description: Some countries are finding themselves forced to buy the most expensive fuel shipments in years.

items.title: Nigeria Gasoline Imports Triple as Dangote Refinery Exports to Earn Dollars
items.description: Nigeria’s gasoline imports more than tripled last month as the country’s main refinery cut domestic supply and increasingly directed output toward export markets to maximize foreign-currency earnings.

items.title: After Nabbing 1,187 Senior-Home Suites, Prima Eyes Its Next Move
items.description: Canadian senior housing operator Prima Living plans to keep expanding through acquisitions and is open to tapping public markets after buying nine retirement communities from Chartwell Retirement Resi

items.title: Norsk Hydro Sees Aluminum Supply Risks as Gulf Flows Deteriorate
items.description: The global aluminum market will face deepening deficits if constraints on shipments through the Strait of Hormuz continue, according to leading European producer Norsk Hydro ASA.

items.title: Namibia Fuel Retailer Nasan Sees Oil Growth After Vitol Deal
items.description: Nasan Energies, a Namibian fuel retailer that recently bought stations from Vitol Group, plans to expand operations to service the country’s offshore oil industry that includes TotalEnergies SE and Sh

items.title: Tanzania Explores Eurobond Sale With Investor Roadshow in London
items.description: Tanzania is gauging investor appetite for its first international bond sale in more than a decade, holding a non-deal roadshow in London on Wednesday.

items.title: Investors Pin Their Hopes on Earnings Breakout
items.description: More S&P 500 companies are raising their outlooks than maintaining or cutting them.

items.title: Stumbling Stock Rally Looks to Earnings for Second Half Boost
items.description: Investors who spent weeks watching US stocks go nowhere are pinning their hopes for a breakout on a key pillar of the four-year bull run: corporate earnings.

items.title: Eternal Revenue More Than Doubles on Quick Commerce Growth
items.description: Eternal Ltd. posted a larger-than-expected increase in quarterly sales, demonstrating its ability to sustain growth despite intense competition in quick commerce that’s sapping margins.

items.title: Prologis Makes £14 Billion Best and Final Offer for Segro
items.description: Prologis Inc. has submitted a best and final offer for Segro Plc that values the UK’s largest publicly traded landlord at about £14 billion ($18.7 billion).

items.title: Kuwait Bond Sale Bids Top $9 Billion Even as Iran Strikes
items.description: Kuwait has drawn more than $9 billion of investor orders for a dollar bond sale on Wednesday, highlighting resilient demand for the OPEC member’s debt even as it comes under daily missile and drone at

items.title: TPG Eyes Sale to Japan Hotel REIT for $860 Million
items.description: Investment fund TPG Asia Real Estate plans to sell a Tokyo hotel property to Japan Hotel REIT Investment Co. as a tourism boom unfolds and boosts valuations, people familiar with the matter said.

items.title: Houthi Threats Give Oil Markets a ‘Two Chokepoint Problem’
items.description: Ships continue to turn away from the southern end of the Red Sea, after Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels announced an embargo on Saudi exports through the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, a narrow channel that

items.title: Paris Wheat Hits Two-Year High as Black Sea Tensions Lift Grains
items.description: Wheat climbed to a two-year high in Paris, as corn extended a rally to the highest level in three years on escalating Black Sea tensions and concerns over crop conditions.

items.title: Yen Rebounds From Four-Decade Low as BOJ May Hike Rates Faster
items.description: The yen weakened past 163 per dollar for the first time since 1986 before recovering some ground on the prospect of faster interest-rate hikes from the Bank of Japan.

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 22
items.description: Oil spiked above $95, extending this month’s price jump to 30%, as the US and Iran played down the prospect of talks and disruptions to global supplies continued to mount.

items.title: Stocks Fall Ahead of Alphabet as Brent Hits $95:  Markets Wrap
items.description: US stocks fell ahead of Alphabet Inc.’s earnings as traders awaited the latest read on whether spending by hyperscalers on artificial intelligence will pay off, while higher oil prices also weighed on

items.title: Moody’s Lifts Argentina Debt Rating in Fresh Boost to Milei
items.description: Argentina’s dollar bonds rose after Moody’s Ratings lifted the country’s credit score, giving the nation its third sovereign upgrade in less than three months in a fresh boost to President Javier Mile
